title: Mutton Rogan Josh
description: Rogan Josh is a signature dish of Kashmiri cuisine, celebrated for its deep red hue, aromatic spices, and rich flavor. Traditionally slow-cooked, it balances tender mutton with layers of spices and yogurt for a velvety, luxurious curry.

ingredients.title: For the Mutton
items: Mutton (goat/lamb, bone-in preferred) – 1 kg, medium-sized pieces
Mustard oil – 120 ml (or ghee for a richer restaurant version)

ingredients.title: For the Spice Base
items: Kashmiri red chili powder – 3 tbsp (for color and mild heat)
Garam masala powder – 1 tsp (freshly ground for best results)

ingredients.title: For Creaminess & Balance
items: Onion paste – 2 medium onions, browned and blended (optional)

ingredients.title: For Finishing
items: Warm water or mutton stock – 600–700 ml
Fresh coriander leaves – small handful (for garnish)
Saffron strands – 8–10, soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Mutton
steps: Wash and pat dry the mutton pieces.
Heat mustard oil until it reaches its smoking point, then reduce the heat.
Fry the mutton pieces in batches until browned and sealed on all sides.
Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: 2. Spice Infusion
steps: In the same oil, add green cardamoms, black cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon stick, and bay leaves.
Cook until the spices become fragrant.
Add asafoetida (hing).
Mix the Kashmiri red chili powder with 2 tbsp warm water and stir it into the pan to prevent burning.

instruction.title: 3. Build the Curry Base
steps: Return the browned mutton to the pan and coat it well with the spiced oil.
Add fennel powder, dry ginger powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt.
Reduce the heat to low.
Gradually stir in the whisked yogurt while continuously mixing to prevent curdling.
If desired, add the browned onion paste for a richer, silkier gravy.

instruction.title: 4. Slow Cook
steps: Pour in the hot water or mutton stock.
Cover and simmer over low heat for 60–90 minutes until the mutton is tender.
Stir occasionally during cooking.
Adjust the gravy to a medium-thick consistency that coats the meat beautifully.

instruction.title: 5. Finish
steps: Stir in the garam masala.
Add the saffron-infused milk if using.
Simmer for another 5 minutes.
Rest the curry for 15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to develop.

instruction.title: Serving Suggestions
steps: Serve traditionally with steamed basmati rice.
Pair with naan, sheermal, or saffron pulao for a restaurant-style meal.
Garnish lightly with fresh coriander leaves.
Avoid adding heavy cream to preserve the authentic Kashmiri flavor.

chefTips: Use bone-in mutton for the richest flavor and a naturally flavorful gravy.
Slow cooking is essential for tender meat and deep flavor development.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides the signature vibrant red color without excessive heat.
Add yogurt gradually over low heat while stirring continuously to prevent splitting.
The curry is perfectly cooked when the oil separates and rises to the surface of the gravy.
